_meta.experiment: E3 + E3-v2 — frozen-embedding probing, GENA-LM vs HyenaDNA
_meta.metric: F1 (mean ± std over 3 seeds, logistic-regression probe on frozen mean-pooled embeddings)
_meta.headline_naive: Naïve last-layer comparison: HyenaDNA wins 3 of 4 tasks with 277× fewer parameters.
_meta.headline_corrected: Read GENA-LM at its best-per-task layer (E1): GENA-LM wins or ties on 3 of 4 tasks.
_meta.narrative: The contrast between these two views is the methodological point of the proposal. The familiar 'big foundation model loses to tiny architecture' story partly reflects a layer-choice artefact: GENA-LM's last hidden layer is pulled toward the MLM objective, not toward downstream biology. E1 showed peak biological signal at L4–L5; reading the embeddings from there flips the head-to-head. The durable guideline is: do not query the last layer if you are not fine-tuning.
scope_caveats: Frozen-probing regime only — authors' fine-tuned numbers are substantially higher (e.g. promoter F1 ≈ 0.94 with 2 kb context).
Single GENA-LM variant (bert-base-t2t, 110.7 M, 4.5 kb max context).
Short-range regulatory tasks only — long-range tasks (where GENA-LM has architectural advantage) are not tested here.
_meta.dnabert2_note: DNABERT-2 was a planned third baseline but failed to load on V100 (transformers compatibility); excluded from comparison.
